entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The third most important food crop, known for resilience under unfavorable conditions and biological nitrification inhibition capacity

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITRIFICATION INHIBITION
entities.description: A plant function in root systems that suppresses soil nitrifier activity to prevent nitrogen loss

entities.name: SOIL NITRIFIER ACTIVITY
entities.description: The activity of soil microbes that convert ammonium to nitrate

entities.name: NITROGEN LOSS
entities.description: The loss of nitrogen from soil through emissions and leaching

entities.name: NITROUS OXIDE
entities.description: A greenhouse gas emitted during nitrogen loss

entities.name: NITRIC OXIDE
entities.description: A gas emitted during nitrogen loss

entities.name: NITRATE
entities.description: A form of nitrogen that can leach into water bodies

entities.name: SORGOLEONE
entities.description: A major hydrophobic phytochemical released from sorghum roots that provides a significant part of BNI function

entities.name: NITRIFYING BACTERIA
entities.description: Bacteria in soil that are suppressed by sorgoleone in pure cultures

entities.name: AMMONIUM
entities.description: A form of nitrogen that is transformed into nitrate and nitrous oxide

entities.name: NITROGEN USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency of nitrogen utilization in plants, which can be increased by high-sorgoleone phenotypes

entities.name: SORGOLEONE SECRETION
entities.description: The process of releasing sorgoleone from roots, which is highly heritable and a target for breeding

entities.name: SORGOLEONE BIOSYNTHESIS
entities.description: The biological process of producing sorgoleone
